{"body":"## Preflight Checklist

- [x] I have searched existing requests and this hasn't been proposed yet

## Problem statement

Gemini 3 series launched March 2026 but the static model catalog only has Gemini 2.x entries. Configuring `gemini-3-flash` in moltis.toml registers the ID but Google API rejects it: "models/gemini-3-flash is not found for API version v1main."

The actual model IDs are versioned (e.g. `gemini-3.0-flash-001`) and need to be in the catalog for correct resolution.

Models needed: `gemini-3-flash` (or actual versioned ID), `gemini-3.1-flash-lite` (or actual versioned ID).

## Proposed solution

Add Gemini 3.x entries to the static catalog with correct Google API model IDs.

## Alternatives considered

Tried `gemini-3-flash` in config — registers but API rejects. No workaround without correct IDs.

## Category

Agent loop / LLM providers

## How important is this to your workflow?

High — significant impact on productivity

## Additional context

Error from logs: `models/gemini-3-flash is not found for API version v1main, or is not supported for generateContent`

Moltis version: 20260408.01","comments":[],"labels":[],"title":"[Feature]: Add Gemini 3.x models to static catalog"}

commit e9a4e492eb8850c003f2fa9b22cd4729c9b0f36f
Author: Fabien Penso <gpg@pen.so>
Date:   Sun Apr 5 16:12:33 2026 +0100

    fix(providers): rediscover models from /v1/models before probing
    
    When "Detect All Models" is triggered, re-query each provider's
    /v1/models (or Ollama /api/tags) endpoint before probing. This finds
    models added to backends like llama.cpp after Moltis started.
    
    Previously detect_supported() only probed models already registered at
    startup, so newly loaded models were invisible until restart.
    
    The fetch phase runs fully async outside the registry lock; only the
    fast in-memory registration takes the write lock.
    
    Closes #551
    
    Entire-Checkpoint: 98d4fd56905b

input.prompt:
Find the Gemini/Google provider implementation in this Rust codebase. I need to understand:

1. Where the static model catalog for Google/Gemini is defined (the list of known model IDs)
2. Whether there's a dynamic model discovery mechanism already implemented (like there is for Anthropic - see commit 7bc43d84)
3. The structure of how models are registered (ModelInfo, recommended flags, etc.)

Search thoroughly in crates/providers/ and related files. Look for "gemini", "google", model lists, and discovery functions. Report file paths and line numbers for all relevant code.

Here is a summary of the exact model ID strings Google uses for the Gemini 3.x series, based on the search results.

## Active Gemini 3.x Model IDs

| Model ID | Display Name | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `gemini-3-flash-preview` | Gemini 3 Flash Preview | Pro-level intelligence at Flash speed/pricing. Has a free tier. |
| `gemini-3.1-pro-preview` | Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview | Latest reasoning-first model for agentic workflows and coding. 1M token context. |
| `gemini-3.1-pro-preview-customtools` | Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview (Custom Tools) | Variant that prioritizes custom tools over bash. |
| `gemini-3.1-flash-lite-preview` | Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite Preview | Most cost-efficient model for high-volume, low-latency tasks. Has a free tier. |
| `gemini-3.1-flash-live-preview` | Gemini 3.1 Flash Live Preview | Low-latency audio-to-audio model for real-time dialogue (Live API). |
| `gemini-3.1-flash-image-preview` | Gemini 3.1 Flash Image Preview | High-efficiency image generation counterpart to Gemini 3 Pro Image. |

## Deprecated / Shut Down

| Model ID | Status |
|---|---|
| `gemini-3-pro-preview` | Shut down March 9, 2026. Replaced by `gemini-3.1-pro-preview`. |

## Key Observations

- Google uses `gemini-3-flash-preview` (no `.0`), not `gemini-3.0-flash` or `gemini-3.0-flash-001`. The format dropped the `.0` and appended `-preview`.
- The 3.1 series uses a dot version: `gemini-3.1-pro-preview`, `gemini-3.1-flash-lite-preview`, etc.
- All Gemini 3.x models are currently in preview status.
- The codebase currently references `gemini-3-flash` as the internal model ID (see `crates/providers/src/lib.rs` and `crates/chat/src/lib.rs`), which would need to map to the API model ID `gemini-3-flash-preview` when making actual API calls.
